steps.action: The polonaise (, French: [pɔlɔnɛz]; Polish: polo with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The polonaise (, French: [pɔlɔnɛz]; Polish: polo with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The original Polish-language name of the dance i
steps.text: The original Polish-language name of the dance is chodzony (pronounced [xɔˈd͡zɔnɨ]), denoting a walking dance.

steps.action: The polonaise dance influenced European ballroom
steps.text: The polonaise dance influenced European ballrooms, folk music and European classical music.

steps.action: The polonaise has a rhythm quite close to that o
steps.text: The polonaise has a rhythm quite close to that of the Swedish semiquaver or sixteenth-note polska, and the two dances share a common origin.

steps.action: Polska dance was introduced to Sweden during the
steps.text: Polska dance was introduced to Sweden during the period of the Vasa dynasty and the Polish–Swedish union.

steps.action: It is the opening dance in major events, at New
steps.text: It is the opening dance in major events, at New Year's balls, on national days as well as other parties.

steps.action: " == Influence of Polonaise in music == The nota
steps.text: " == Influence of Polonaise in music == The notation alla polacca (Italian: polacca means "polonaise") on a musical score indicates that the piece should be played with the rhythm and character of a polonaise.

steps.action: 2 is marked "Alla Polacca", his Horn Concertino
steps.text: 2 is marked "Alla Polacca", his Horn Concertino likewise ends with a polka movement, and the finale of Chopin's Variations on "Là ci darem la mano" both feature this notation.

steps.text: 8, marked "Allegretto alla Polacca," as a representative example of the polonaise dance topic (Ratner 1980, pp.

steps.action: John Philip Sousa wrote the Presidential Polonai
steps.text: John Philip Sousa wrote the Presidential Polonaise, intended to keep visitors moving briskly through the White House receiving line.

steps.action: Polonaise originated as a peasant dance known un
steps.text: Polonaise originated as a peasant dance known under various names – chodzony ("pacer"), chmielowy ("hops"), pieszy ("walker") or wielki ("great"), recorded as early as the 15th century.

steps.action: By the late 16th century, the folk versions of p
steps.text: By the late 16th century, the folk versions of polonaise (accompanied by singing) were commonly danced by the lower Polish nobility, but the dance was not known under its current name until the 17th century.

steps.action: With time, it also became a favorite dance of th
steps.text: With time, it also became a favorite dance of the Polish aristocratic class and acquired an instrumental form.

steps.action: This dance in 3/4 meter was designed to entertai
steps.text: This dance in 3/4 meter was designed to entertain the French royal court.

steps.action: A loosely defined group dance, it typically rese
steps.text: A loosely defined group dance, it typically resembles a country dance or cèilidh, or in some cases a line dance.

steps.action: The polonaise is popular in Poland today.
steps.text: The polonaise is popular in Poland today.

steps.text: For example, the third movement of Beethoven's Triple Concerto op.

steps.action: Frédéric Chopin's polonaises are generally the b
steps.text: Frédéric Chopin's polonaises are generally the best known of all polonaises in classical music.

steps.action: But there was a long tradition of polonaise in E
steps.text: But there was a long tradition of polonaise in European music at least 100 years before Chopin.

steps.action: Händel wrote a famous one, and Wilhelm Fiedemann
steps.text: Händel wrote a famous one, and Wilhelm Fiedemann Bach wrote a number of beautiful ones in major minor pairs.

steps.action: Another more recent prolific polonaise composer
steps.text: Another more recent prolific polonaise composer was the American Edward Alexander MacDowell.
